words.example: She felt scared during the thunderstorm.
He was worried about the exam results.
Kids love to pretend they are superheroes.
She complained about the noisy neighbor.
He seemed tired after the long trip.
Why don't you come over for dinner?
He finally came to understand the problem.
Whoose book is this on the table?
You should drink at least eight glasses of water.

words.portuguese: em vez de / prefiro / bastante / antes / preferivelmente
words.example: I would rather stay home tonight.
Despite the rain, the match continued.

words.portuguese: justamente / razoalvelmente / bastante / de maneira justa
words.example: She played fairly and won the game.
Gotcha! I was just kidding.
It's sort of weird, but I like it.

words.portuguese: bricando / zoando / bobear /desperdiçar tempo / não fazer nada produtivo
words.example: Stop goofing off and finish your work.

words.portuguese: dê uma olhada / ter um vislumbre
words.example: I got a glimpse of the sunrise from my window.
The project is so far so good, keep it up.
Look out! The bike is coming fast.

words.portuguese: Usado para indicar as horas inteiras no relógio (abreviação de "of the clock")
words.example: The meeting starts at three o'clock.

words.portuguese: Mais distante, mais adiante ou mais
words.example: We need to discuss this further tomorrow.
That movie had a weird ending.
The library is a quiet place to study.
She was quite surprised by the news.
He decided to quit his job yesterday.
She hopes to achieve her goals soon.
Watch out for the gotchas in the contract.
Would you like another cup of coffee?
She is aware of the upcoming deadline.
The vaccine was a major breakthrough.

words.portuguese: revisar / melhorar algo que já se sabe
words.example: I need to brush up on my Spanish.
We walked along the quiet beach at sunset.

words.portuguese: cadela (literal) / usado também como um insulto (gíria)
words.example: She called him a bitter bitch in anger.
The exam was tough but fair.

words.portuguese: embora / porém (no final da frase, dá ideia de mas)
words.example: It was cold, though we went outside.
That thought crossed my mind yesterday.
She walked through the tunnel quickly.
She gave a thorough explanation of the process.
The news spread throughout the city.
She brought cookies to the meeting.
He bought a new laptop yesterday.

words.portuguese: regra / norma ou princípio que deve ser seguido
words.example: The rule states that late arrivals are penalized.

words.portuguese: função ou posição que alguém desempenha em um contexto específico
words.example: She played the lead role in the play.

words.portuguese: valor ou importância de algo ou alguém
words.example: The painting is worth millions of dollars.
He is claiming ownership of the patent.
Actually, I prefer tea over coffee.
In fact, the meeting starts at nine.
Take the bus instead of driving.
Since you’re here, we can start.
The game was cancelled due to rain.
It rained; therefore, the match was postponed.
I'll start cooking; in the meantime, set the table.
Never mind, I found the keys myself.
What a pity that you missed the concert.
We'll be landing pretty soon, hold on.
Hopefully, the weather will improve tomorrow.
Will you join us? You bet!
When the puzzle clicked, I said, I got it.
She laughed as if she knew the secret.
Wake up early to catch the sunrise.
How come you're late again?
You stepped on my foot—my bad.
Besides pizza, we ordered salad.
Please throw away the empty bottles.
The teacher has a strict grading policy.
No way I'm missing the final game!
The forest was home to many wild animals.
The kitten is very playful with yarn.

words.portuguese: em relação a / a respeito de / sobre
words.example: She sent an email regarding the meeting.
The success of the project relies on teamwork.
All his hard work pays off in the end.
His greatest weakness is impatience.
Yoga contributes to overall wellness.
She managed to accomplish her goals.
As far as I know, the meeting is tomorrow.
As far as it goes, the plan works.
We need to narrow down the options.
The buzzing fly was really annoying.
The downside of the job is the long commute.
He can be naive about people's motives.
She is a reliable source of information.
The AI uses a reasoning engine to solve puzzles.
The hero finally confronted his enemy.
The bride walked down the aisle.
The groom waited nervously at the altar.
The brave soldier earned many medals.
She was afraid of the dark.
He always stirs the pot during meetings.
He wore a green beret during the ceremony.
The new law was strictly enforced.
Every product has its drawbacks.
Electricity powers most modern devices.

words.portuguese: algo de baixa qualidade ou mal feito relacionado à inteligência artificial
words.example: The article was pure AI slop, no insight.
She loves to craft handmade jewelry.
The boat moved at a steady pace.
They plan to raise funds for the charity.
The kids played in the dirt after school.

words.portuguese: expressão de surpresa ou medo, algo como "caramba" ou "nossa"
words.example: Yikes, that spider is huge!

sentences.english: I don't think that the picnic is over yet.
sentences.portuguese: Eu não acho que o piquenique acabou ainda.

sentences.english: Let's face it.
sentences.portuguese: Vamos ser sinceros.

sentences.english: Won't you please decide soon if you're coming ?
sentences.portuguese: Por favor, você não vai decidir logo se vai vir?

sentences.english: Why can't the students walk on the dirt ?
sentences.portuguese: Por que os alunos não podem andar na terra?

sentences.english: Everyone's on equal footing here.
sentences.portuguese: Todos estão em pé de igualdade aqui.

sentences.english: Where can't visitors stand ?
sentences.portuguese: Onde os visitantes não podem ficar ?

sentences.english: That person said the newspaper was theirs
sentences.portuguese: Aquela pessoa disse que o jornal era deles

sentences.english: Sometimes being friendly with everyone pays off.
sentences.portuguese: Às vezes ser amigável com todos compensa.

sentences.english: Have you guys ever gone camping ?
sentences.portuguese: Vocês já foram acampar ?

sentences.english: He gave her a kiss on the cheek
sentences.portuguese: Ele deu um beijo na bochecha dela

sentences.english: Which bowl would you like your ice cream in ?
sentences.portuguese: Em qual tigela gostaria do seu sorvete ?

sentences.english: Am I coming through ok ?
sentences.portuguese: Está me escutando ?

sentences.english: People are making fun of me for hugging my friends. They said it's too much.
sentences.portuguese: As pessoas estão tirando sarro de mim por abraçar meus amigos. Dizem que é demais.

sentences.english: Do you want to know something even cooler?
sentences.portuguese: Você quer saber de algo ainda mais legal?

sentences.english: Have all the students finished reviewing the lesson yet ?
sentences.portuguese: Todos os alunos já terminaram de rever a lição?

sentences.english: Have the kids fallen asleep yet ?
sentences.portuguese: As crianças já caíram no sono?

sentences.english: Have you ever fallen asleep in class ?
sentences.portuguese: Você caiu no sono na aula alguma vez?

sentences.english: Only six weeks' worth of research data
sentences.portuguese: Apenas seis semanas de dados de pesquisa

sentences.english: Don't be a jerk
sentences.portuguese: Não seja um idiota

sentences.english: In Ireland, it's nice to bring flowers when you go to someone's house.
sentences.portuguese: Na Irlanda, é simpático levar flores quando você vai a casa de alguém.

sentences.english: it's nice to meet you
sentences.portuguese: É um prazer conhecê-lo

sentences.english: Does anyone know what monkeys use stones for ?
sentences.portuguese: Alguém sabe para que os macacos usam pedras?

sentences.english: Quit shouting the teacher is mad at us.
sentences.portuguese: Pare de gritar, o professor está bravo com a gente.
